jokes.text: watch Richard Simmons so my body will be lean and supple for your return
jokes.setup: Simka describing how she'll spend her day making their home perfect
jokes.punchline: Including watching Richard Simmons for physical fitness in an overly earnest, romantic way
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific absurdity - only Simka would earnestly include Richard Simmons in romantic preparation

jokes.text: And I shall puke my guts out... if you don't knock it off
jokes.setup: Romantic declarations between Latka and Simka
jokes.punchline: Louie's visceral disgust expressed in characteristically crude terms
jokes.explanation: Perfect Louie response - crude, direct, and cutting through romantic sentiment with characteristic bluntness

jokes.text: A green card?
jokes.setup: Simka says Louie doesn't have what they have
jokes.punchline: Alex's practical assumption about what foreign couple has that Louie doesn't
jokes.explanation: Excellent economy and misdirection - two words that completely reframe the romantic setup

jokes.text: Shloogel.
jokes.setup: Latka introducing the foreign concept of 'shloogel'
jokes.punchline: Louie's flat, skeptical repetition of the absurd-sounding word
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective reaction comedy playing on the absurdity of the made-up word

jokes.text: Comes to us naturally as eating, sleeping and disemboweling sheep
jokes.setup: Latka explaining his natural gift for matchmaking
jokes.punchline: Escalating from normal activities to grotesquely specific violence
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation structure - starts normal then veers into shocking specificity that's perfectly in character

jokes.text: See, this shows how good Simka is. That's exactly what your date said
jokes.setup: Alex gives philosophical speech about individual choice and romance
jokes.punchline: Reveals his date said the exact same thing, undermining his uniqueness
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection that deflates Alex's intellectual posturing by revealing perfect matchmaking worked

jokes.text: It ain't this meatball
jokes.setup: Tony asks Vicki to describe her perfect man after he describes wanting someone with great personality
jokes.punchline: Immediate brutal rejection with colorful insult
jokes.explanation: Sharp, economical putdown that immediately establishes the mismatch dynamic

jokes.text: So they found you one
jokes.setup: Description of Elaine usually dating handsome rogues and lady killers
jokes.punchline: Implying Arnie is exactly that type when he clearly isn't
jokes.explanation: Good ironic delivery - the mismatch between description and reality is the joke

jokes.text: Oh!
jokes.setup: Jim being asked about favorite TV show, says Bob Newhart Show
jokes.punchline: His shocked recognition when he sees his date is actually from the show
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jim moment - his genuine surprise and delight at the surreal situation

jokes.text: I wanted a date
jokes.setup: Question about how they got such a big star for Jim's date
jokes.punchline: Brutally honest, deadpan admission of loneliness
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-humor - a real actress playing herself with self-deprecating honesty

jokes.text: How come my date's blind? / Glaucoma
jokes.setup: Louie's suspicious question about his date being blind
jokes.punchline: Latka's matter-of-fact medical explanation missing the point entirely
jokes.explanation: Perfect cross-purposes humor - Louie suspects insult, Latka gives literal medical answer

jokes.text: The thought did cross my mind.
jokes.setup: Asked if he thinks Simka picked a blind girl so she couldn't see him
jokes.punchline: Louie's matter-of-fact admission of his own unattractiveness
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Louie's surprising self-awareness about his appearance

jokes.text: Hello, grandchildren
jokes.setup: Latka recording first words for potential grandchildren
jokes.punchline: Alex addressing future grandchildren directly in his first meeting
jokes.explanation: Clever absurdist moment - Alex playing along with the ridiculous premise in an unexpected way

jokes.text: No, I'm serious
jokes.setup: Judy says she made her outfit herself
jokes.punchline: Louie doubling down on his insult, making it worse
jokes.explanation: Perfectly awful Louie behavior - making a bad situation worse through persistence

jokes.text: Archery?
jokes.setup: Judy saying blind people can do all sorts of things
jokes.punchline: Louie's suggestion of the one activity that seems most impossible for blind people
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor - one word that perfectly captures Louie's inappropriate mindset

jokes.text: At the moment, uh, I'm sitting here, uh, picturing you, uh, naked
jokes.setup: Susan asking Alex to tell her about himself
jokes.punchline: Alex's completely inappropriate and uncharacteristic directness
jokes.explanation: Great character reversal - normally reasonable Alex being shockingly forward in desperation

jokes.text: Ah! [Carol's signature laugh]
jokes.setup: Jim asking her to do the laugh from the Christmas show
jokes.punchline: The actual distinctive laugh from The Bob Newhart Show
jokes.explanation: Good meta moment - actual actress performing her famous character's signature element

jokes.text: And you never won an Emmy?
jokes.setup: Marcia Wallace doing her signature laugh from the show
jokes.punchline: Jim's incredulous reaction to the real actress not winning awards for the performance
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-commentary on TV industry while staying true to Jim's obsessive fan character

jokes.text: And half the football team
jokes.setup: Tony describing his loving relationship with siblings
jokes.punchline: Unexpected sexual implication that he immediately backtracks from
jokes.explanation: Standard misdirection humor - sets up wholesome family talk then veers into inappropriate territory

jokes.text: No, it's because I'm incredibly rich
jokes.setup: Susan guessing why women are attracted to Alex (listening, caring about children)
jokes.punchline: Alex's blunt materialism after seeming so sensitive
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection playing against romantic expectations and Alex's earlier vulnerability

jokes.text: Woody Allen has conditioned us to reevaluate our concept of male beauty
jokes.setup: Susan explaining why she finds Alex attractive
jokes.punchline: Pseudo-intellectual explanation that's both flattering and insulting
jokes.explanation: Clever cultural reference that works as both compliment and backhanded insult

jokes.text: Suddenly I'm afraid of dying
jokes.setup: Arnie describing how meeting Elaine has changed his perspective
jokes.punchline: The morbid way he expresses newfound appreciation for life
jokes.explanation: Dark but effective romantic sentiment - expressing love through fear of death is uniquely neurotic

jokes.text: More wine? / Ah, no thanks. I'll just finish what I have in my glass. / Oh, okay.
jokes.setup: Louie offering wine to his blind date
jokes.punchline: The visual of her accurately knowing her glass contents despite being blind, making Louie look foolish
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy - Judy's competence contrasts with Louie's assumptions about blindness

jokes.text: Good ears, good ears
jokes.setup: Judy demonstrating she knows exactly how much wine is in her glass
jokes.punchline: Louie's patronizing and inappropriate response
jokes.explanation: Perfectly awful Louie moment - condescending praise that reveals his ignorance about blindness

jokes.text: I would be honored if you would become my wife
jokes.setup: Jim asking if he can ask Marcia another question
jokes.punchline: Immediately jumping to marriage proposal on first meeting
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jim absurdity - his childlike directness and the massive escalation from casual question

jokes.text: I hit her, I hit her
jokes.setup: Tony showing Vicki his boxing stance
jokes.punchline: Accidentally hitting his date while demonstrating
jokes.explanation: Standard physical comedy - predictable outcome but well-executed for Tony's character

jokes.text: I'm lucky you're a bum
jokes.setup: Tony accidentally hitting Vicki during boxing demonstration
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment that he's lucky his punch was weak
jokes.explanation: Sharp comeback that turns the situation around - she's insulting his boxing ability while being hit

jokes.text: Before you know it, she'll be taking me home to meet the dog
jokes.setup: Louie and Judy saying they might get serious
jokes.punchline: Dog instead of parents - both because she's blind and Louie's low status
jokes.explanation: Clever double meaning - plays on blind people having guide dogs and Louie's low social status

jokes.text: ♪ Here comes Bob and Carol ♪ ♪ His wife Emily really likes him ♪
jokes.setup: Jim offering to sing his lyric to Marcia's theme song
jokes.punchline: Completely wrong character names and simplistic lyrics
jokes.explanation: Good Jim confusion - gets the character names wrong despite being obsessed with the show

jokes.text: I think we're all going to die
jokes.setup: Latka celebrating how well the shloogel went
jokes.punchline: Simka's sudden existential despair
jokes.explanation: Brilliant mood whiplash - from celebration to existential dread in perfect character voice

jokes.text: I think we're all going to die. I think love is an illusion.
jokes.setup: Latka celebrating the success of the shloogel
jokes.punchline: Simka's sudden existential crisis and pessimistic outlook
jokes.explanation: Great tonal shift that subverts the romantic success with dark philosophical despair

jokes.text: Well, ordinarily I'd ask you for your phone number, but I'd feel kind of silly asking for that now, so I'll just... settle for the first four digits
jokes.setup: Alex trying to get Susan's number after his earlier disasters
jokes.punchline: Self-deprecating approach that acknowledges his reduced chances
jokes.explanation: Clever self-aware humor - Alex acknowledging his failure while still trying to succeed

jokes.text: 5552... And the last three are 437
jokes.setup: Alex's reduced expectations asking for only first four digits
jokes.punchline: Susan giving him the full number anyway
jokes.explanation: Sweet payoff to Alex's self-deprecating approach - she rewards his honesty with success
